question: Which player has won the most Ballon d'Or awards?
explanation: Lionel Messi has won 8 Ballon d'Or awards (2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2015, 2019, 2021, 2023), more than any other player in history.

question: Which country has won the most FIFA World Cups?
explanation: Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup 5 times (1958, 1962, 1966, 1970, 1994, 2002 — actually 5 times). They are the only team to have competed in every tournament.

question: Which club has won the UEFA Champions League the most times?
explanation: Real Madrid has won the Champions League 15 times, including three consecutive titles under Zidane (2016, 2017, 2018) and their 15th in 2024 against Borussia Dortmund.

question: Which club did Cristiano Ronaldo NOT play for?
explanation: Ronaldo's career path: Sporting CP → Manchester United → Real Madrid → Juventus → Manchester United → Al-Nassr. He never played for PSG.

question: Who scored the 'Goal of the Century' in the 1986 World Cup quarter-final against England?
explanation: Maradona dribbled past five England players and the goalkeeper before scoring. In the same match, he also scored the infamous 'Hand of God' goal, which he later admitted was intentional.

question: Which African country became the first to reach a FIFA World Cup semi-final?
explanation: Morocco reached the semi-finals of the 2022 Qatar World Cup, beating Portugal in the quarter-finals. They lost to France but beat Croatia in the third-place play-off match.

question: Who scored the winning goal for Spain in the 2010 FIFA World Cup final?
explanation: Andres Iniesta scored in the 116th minute of extra time to give Spain a 1-0 victory over the Netherlands. It was Spain's first ever World Cup title.

question: Which year was the UEFA Champions League rebranded from the European Cup?
explanation: The European Cup was rebranded as the UEFA Champions League in 1992, introducing a group stage format. The first Champions League was won by Marseille in 1993.

question: Which club plays their home games at the Allianz Arena?
explanation: The Allianz Arena in Munich has been Bayern Munich's home since 2005. It is famous for its illuminated outer facade which can change colour — red for Bayern, blue for the German national team.

question: How many maximum substitutions are allowed per team in a standard FIFA match?
explanation: FIFA increased the maximum number of substitutions from 3 to 5 in 2020, initially as a temporary measure during the COVID-19 pandemic. It became permanent in 2021.

question: Pelé is the only footballer to have won the FIFA World Cup three times.
explanation: Pelé won the World Cup in 1958, 1962, and 1970 with Brazil. Although he was injured in the 1962 tournament, he was part of the squad and is officially a three-time World Cup winner.

question: Cristiano Ronaldo has never won the FIFA World Cup.
explanation: Portugal, despite having Ronaldo in their squad, have never won the FIFA World Cup. Their best finish was third place in 1966 with Eusébio.

question: The first FIFA World Cup was held in Uruguay in 1930.
explanation: Uruguay hosted and won the inaugural FIFA World Cup in 1930. They beat Argentina 4-2 in the final in Montevideo. Only 13 teams participated.

question: Lionel Messi won the FIFA World Cup with Argentina in 2022.
explanation: Argentina defeated France 4-2 on penalties (3-3 AET) in the 2022 Qatar World Cup final. It was Messi's first World Cup title after four previous unsuccessful attempts.

question: The penalty spot is 11 yards from the goal line.
explanation: The penalty spot is 12 yards (11 metres) from the goal line, not 11 yards. A common misconception — it is 11 metres but 12 yards.

question: Brazil is the only country to have appeared in every FIFA World Cup tournament.
explanation: Brazil has qualified for every FIFA World Cup since the competition began in 1930 — the only country to do so. They have appeared in all 22 tournaments held up to 2022.

question: A standard FIFA football pitch must be exactly 105 metres long.
explanation: FIFA regulations allow a range: 100–110 metres in length and 64–75 metres in width for international matches. There is no single fixed dimension — though 105m × 68m is the standard recommended size.

question: Zinedine Zidane was sent off in his final professional match.
explanation: In the 2006 World Cup final against Italy, Zidane headbutted Marco Materazzi in the chest in extra time and received a straight red card. France lost on penalties. It was his final professional appearance.

question: Real Madrid won three consecutive UEFA Champions League titles between 2016 and 2018.
explanation: Under manager Zinedine Zidane, Real Madrid became the first club to win three consecutive Champions League titles (2016, 2017, 2018) in the modern format.

question: Kylian Mbappé scored a hat-trick in the 2022 World Cup final but still ended up on the losing side.
explanation: Mbappé scored 3 goals for France in the final (1 in 90 mins, 2 in extra time) to make it 3-3. France still lost 4-2 on penalties, making him the second player to score a hat-trick in a World Cup final after Geoff Hurst in 1966.

question: The trophy awarded to the top scorer at a FIFA World Cup is called the Golden ___.
explanation: The Golden Boot is awarded to the player who scores the most goals at a World Cup. If players are tied on goals, assists are used as a tiebreaker, then minutes played.

question: Manchester United's home ground is called Old ___.
explanation: Old Trafford, nicknamed 'The Theatre of Dreams' by Bobby Charlton, has been Manchester United's home since 1910. It currently holds around 74,000 spectators.

question: The annual award for the world's best football player, voted for by journalists, is called the ___ d'Or.
explanation: The Ballon d'Or ('Golden Ball' in French) has been awarded by France Football magazine since 1956. Lionel Messi holds the record with 8 awards.

question: When a player scores three goals in a single match, it is called a ___.
explanation: The term 'hat-trick' originated in cricket in the 1850s before being adopted by football. In football, the three goals must be scored by the same player, but not necessarily consecutively.

question: Diego Maradona's infamous '___  of God' goal was scored against England in the 1986 World Cup.
explanation: Maradona punched the ball into the net with his left hand in the 51st minute. The Tunisian referee didn't spot it and allowed the goal. Maradona later described it as 'a little with the head of Maradona and a little with the hand of God.'

question: Cristiano Ronaldo is famous for his goal celebration that ends with the exclamation '___!'
explanation: Ronaldo's 'Siuuu!' celebration started around 2013. He jumps, spins, and lands with his arms wide open shouting 'Siuuu!' — a Spanish exclamation of joy. Fans worldwide have adopted it.

question: The football governing body in Europe is called ___.
explanation: UEFA (Union of European Football Associations) was founded in 1954 and governs football across 55 member associations in Europe. FIFA governs the sport globally.

question: A goal scored directly from a corner kick — without any other player touching the ball — is called an ___ goal.
explanation: An 'Olympic goal' or 'gol olímpico' is when a goal is scored directly from a corner kick. The term originated in South America after Cesáreo Onzari scored such a goal for Argentina against Uruguay (the Olympic champions) in 1924.

question: In football, when a referee raises both arms straight above their head, they are signalling a ___.
explanation: A referee signals a penalty kick by pointing to the penalty spot. Both arms raised indicates play is still live after a foul — actually in modern officiating, the raised arm typically indicates an advantage or indirect free-kick. A penalty is signalled by pointing to the spot.

question: The FC Barcelona youth academy, one of the most famous in the world, is called La ___.
explanation: La Masia ('The Farmhouse' in Catalan) has produced some of football's greatest players including Lionel Messi, Xavi, Andres Iniesta, Pep Guardiola, Cesc Fàbregas, and Gerard Piqué.

question: Rank these countries by number of FIFA World Cup wins (most to least):
explanation: Brazil (5) → Germany (4) → Argentina (3). Brazil won in 1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, 2002. Germany won in 1954, 1974, 1990, 2014. Argentina won in 1978, 1986, 2022.

question: Rank these players by number of Ballon d'Or awards (most to least):
explanation: Messi (8) → Ronaldo (5) → Platini (3, consecutive 1983–85). Messi's 8th came in 2023.

question: Rank these FIFA World Cup host nations in chronological order (earliest first):
explanation: South Africa (2010) → Brazil (2014) → Qatar (2022). South Africa was the first African nation to host the World Cup.

question: Rank these clubs by number of UEFA Champions League titles (most to least):
explanation: Real Madrid (15) → AC Milan (7) → Liverpool (6). Real Madrid's 15th title came in 2024 against Borussia Dortmund.

question: Rank these transfers from most to least expensive (at time of transfer):
explanation: Neymar to PSG €222m (world record) → Ronaldo to Juventus €100m → Bale to Real Madrid €91m (was a world record at the time).

question: Rank these European football leagues by average match attendance (highest first):
explanation: Bundesliga (~43,000) → Premier League (~39,000) → La Liga (~28,000). Despite having the highest broadcast revenues, La Liga has fewer large stadiums and lower average attendances.

question: Rank these managers by number of UEFA Champions League titles won (most to least):
explanation: Ancelotti (5: 2003, 2007 with Milan, 2014, 2022, 2024 with Real Madrid) → Guardiola (3: 2009, 2011 with Barcelona, 2023 with Man City) → Ferguson (2: 1999, 2008 with Man Utd).

question: Rank these England footballing moments in chronological order (earliest first):
explanation: 1966 World Cup win (beat West Germany 4-2 at Wembley) → 2018 World Cup 4th place (lost to Belgium in 3rd place match) → Euro 2020 final (lost 3-2 on penalties to Italy, played in 2021).

question: Rank these football clubs by their founding year (oldest first):
explanation: Sheffield FC (1857) is the world's oldest football club. Notts County (1862) is the oldest Football League club. Stoke City (1863) is one of the oldest professional clubs.

question: Rank these Ballon d'Or winners in the order they won the award (earliest first):
explanation: Zinedine Zidane (1998) → Ronaldo R9 (2002) → Ronaldinho (2005). Note: Ronaldo (the Brazilian) also won the Ballon d'Or in 1997, before Zidane.

question: How many goals did Lionel Messi score in La Liga during his record-breaking 2011-12 season?
explanation: Messi scored 50 La Liga goals in the 2011-12 season, breaking Gerd Müller's European record of 40 goals in a single Bundesliga season. He also scored 73 goals in all competitions that season.

question: How many times has Brazil won the FIFA World Cup?
explanation: Brazil won the World Cup in 1958 (Sweden), 1962 (Chile), 1970 (Mexico), 1994 (USA), and 2002 (Japan/South Korea). They remain the most successful nation with 5 titles.

question: How many UEFA Champions League titles has Real Madrid won in total?
explanation: Real Madrid won their 15th Champions League title in 2024, beating Borussia Dortmund 2-0 at Wembley. They are far ahead of the second-most successful club, AC Milan, with 7.

question: In what year was FIFA (Fédération Internationale de Football Association) founded?
explanation: FIFA was founded on 21 May 1904 in Paris, France, with 7 founding member nations: France, Belgium, Denmark, the Netherlands, Spain, Sweden, and Switzerland. Great Britain did not join until 1905.

question: What is the world record transfer fee ever paid for a single player, in millions of euros?
explanation: Neymar's transfer from FC Barcelona to Paris Saint-Germain in 2017 for €222 million remains the most expensive transfer in football history, nearly doubling the previous record.

question: In what year was the Premier League founded?
explanation: The Premier League was founded in 1992 when the top clubs broke away from the Football League. The first season (1992-93) was won by Manchester United. Before this, the top division was called the First Division.

question: How many teams competed in the 2022 FIFA World Cup?
explanation: 32 teams competed at the 2022 Qatar World Cup. From 2026 onward, the World Cup will expand to 48 teams across the USA, Canada, and Mexico.

question: What is the highest margin of victory ever recorded in a full international football match?
explanation: Australia beat American Samoa 31-0 in a 2002 World Cup qualifying match, the largest margin of victory in international football history. Archie Thompson scored 13 goals in that match, also a record.

question: How long is a standard football match in minutes (regular time, excluding stoppage time)?
explanation: A standard football match consists of two 45-minute halves = 90 minutes of regular time. Stoppage time is added at the end of each half for interruptions.

question: How many players does each team have on the pitch at the start of a match?
explanation: Each team fields 11 players: typically 1 goalkeeper and 10 outfield players. A team that drops below 7 players due to red cards or injuries must forfeit the match.

question: Which country does Cristiano Ronaldo play for?
explanation: Cristiano Ronaldo has represented Portugal since 2003. He is Portugal's all-time leading scorer and holds the record for most international goals by a male player.

question: What color card is shown to send a player off immediately?
explanation: A red card means immediate expulsion from the match. It can be given for serious foul play, violent conduct, a second yellow card offence, or denying a goal-scoring opportunity.

question: Which club is nicknamed 'Los Blancos' (The Whites)?
explanation: Real Madrid are nicknamed 'Los Blancos' because of their all-white kit, adopted in 1902. The white strip is said to have been inspired by Corinthian FC, an amateur English club.

question: Which player is nicknamed 'La Pulga' (The Flea)?
explanation: Lionel Messi earned the nickname 'La Pulga' (The Flea) due to his small stature (1.70m) and his ability to dart past defenders with incredible speed and agility.

question: Which country does Kylian Mbappé represent internationally?
explanation: Kylian Mbappé plays for France, despite having Cameroonian heritage through his father. He made his debut at 18 and was a key player in France's 2018 World Cup victory.

question: How long is each half of a standard football match?
explanation: Each half of a football match is 45 minutes, making 90 minutes of regular time. Stoppage time (injury time) is added to compensate for delays during play.

question: What is the name of the famous rivalry match between FC Barcelona and Real Madrid?
explanation: 'El Clásico' is the match between FC Barcelona and Real Madrid, widely considered the biggest club fixture in world football. 'Der Klassiker' is the Bayern Munich vs Borussia Dortmund rivalry.

question: Which position does the player wearing the number 1 shirt traditionally play?
explanation: The number 1 shirt is traditionally worn by the goalkeeper. In modern football, squad numbers are often fixed, but number 1 is still almost universally reserved for the first-choice goalkeeper.

question: Which club is known as 'La Vecchia Signora' (The Old Lady)?
explanation: Juventus FC is nicknamed 'La Vecchia Signora' (The Old Lady) due to their long history and traditional status in Italian football. They are the most successful club in Serie A history.

question: What does 'VAR' stand for in football?
explanation: VAR stands for Video Assistant Referee. It was introduced by FIFA to review match-changing decisions: goals, penalty decisions, direct red cards, and mistaken identity.

question: What is the name of FC Barcelona's iconic home stadium?
explanation: Camp Nou ('New Field' in Catalan) has been Barcelona's home since 1957. With a capacity of around 99,000, it is the largest stadium in Europe.

question: What is the nickname of the French national football team?
explanation: France's national team is called 'Les Bleus' (The Blues), after their traditional blue jerseys. France have won the World Cup twice (1998, 2018) and the Euros twice (1984, 2000).

question: Which manager is nicknamed 'The Special One'?
explanation: José Mourinho coined the phrase himself at his first Chelsea press conference in 2004: 'Please don't call me arrogant, but I'm a European champion and I think I'm a special one.'

question: In which city is the Santiago Bernabéu stadium located?
explanation: The Santiago Bernabéu is located in Madrid, Spain, and has been Real Madrid's home since 1947. It is named after the club's former president who oversaw its construction.

question: Which country won the inaugural FIFA World Cup in 1930?
explanation: Uruguay beat Argentina 4-2 in the first World Cup final, played in Montevideo on July 30, 1930. Uruguay were also the reigning Olympic champions at the time.

question: What does the abbreviation 'FIFA' stand for?
explanation: FIFA stands for Fédération Internationale de Football Association — the French name for the International Federation of Association Football. French was chosen as the official language at its 1904 founding.

question: Who broke Pelé's record as Brazil's all-time top goal scorer in 2023?
explanation: Neymar scored his 78th Brazil goal in November 2023, surpassing Pelé's record of 77 official international goals. Pelé acknowledged the achievement warmly before his death in December 2022.

question: What nationality is manager Pep Guardiola?
explanation: Pep Guardiola was born in Santpedor, Catalonia, Spain. As a player he was a key part of Johan Cruyff's 'Dream Team' Barcelona. As a manager he is widely considered the greatest of his generation.

question: What is the name of the derby match played between Arsenal and Tottenham Hotspur?
explanation: The North London Derby between Arsenal and Tottenham is one of the most fierce local rivalries in English football. The two clubs are just a few miles apart in north London.

question: Name the famous FC Barcelona youth academy that produced Messi, Xavi, and Iniesta.
explanation: La Masia ('The Farmhouse') is Barcelona's world-renowned youth academy, founded in 1979. It has produced some of the greatest players ever, including Messi, Xavi, Iniesta, Fàbregas, Piqué, Puyol, and Busquets.

question: Who am I?
clues: I am widely considered the greatest footballer of all time, having scored over 1,000 goals in my career
I am the only player in history to have won the FIFA World Cup three times
In 1958, I became the youngest player to score in a World Cup final at just 17 years old
My real name is Edson Arantes do Nascimento, but the world knows me by my nickname
explanation: Pelé played for Santos FC and New York Cosmos, wearing the iconic #10 shirt for Brazil. He passed away in December 2022 at age 82.

question: Who am I?
clues: I was born in Rosario, Argentina, and moved to FC Barcelona's youth academy at age 13 after they agreed to pay for my growth hormone treatment
I have won the Ballon d'Or more times than any other player in history
My nickname is 'La Pulga' (The Flea) due to my small stature and quick movement
I finally won the FIFA World Cup with Argentina in Qatar in 2022, completing my collection of every major trophy
explanation: Messi currently plays for Inter Miami after leaving PSG in 2023. He won 8 Ballon d'Ors, 4 Champions Leagues, 10 La Liga titles, and the 2022 World Cup.

question: Who am I?
clues: I was born on the island of Madeira, Portugal, in 1985, and grew up in difficult circumstances
I made my name at Manchester United before becoming the most expensive player in history at the time with my move to Real Madrid in 2009
I am one of two players to have won five or more Ballon d'Or awards
My famous goal celebrations always conclude with a mid-air spin and the exclamation 'Siuuu!'
explanation: Ronaldo is one of the greatest footballers ever. He holds the record for most goals in Champions League history and most international goals by a male player.

question: Who am I?
clues: I was born in Marseille, France, to Algerian immigrant parents, and grew up in the tough La Castellane estate
In the 1998 World Cup final on home soil, I scored two headed goals to beat Brazil 3-0 and deliver France their first World Cup
I won the Ballon d'Or in 1998 and am regarded as one of the most technically gifted players of all time
In my very last professional match — the 2006 World Cup final — I was sent off for headbutting Marco Materazzi in the chest
explanation: Zidane later became Real Madrid manager, winning three consecutive Champions League titles (2016, 2017, 2018). His elegance and vision made him one of the most celebrated players in history.

question: Who am I?
clues: I am a Spanish manager born in Santpedor, Catalonia, who played as a deep-lying midfielder for Johan Cruyff's Barcelona 'Dream Team'
As a manager, I developed the 'tiki-taka' possession-based style to perfection, winning the treble in my first season at Barcelona
I have managed three of the biggest clubs in world football: FC Barcelona, Bayern Munich, and Manchester City
I won the Champions League in 2009, 2011, and 2023 — the latter finally ending my long wait for the trophy in England
explanation: Guardiola is widely regarded as the best manager of his generation. His Man City side of 2022-23 became the first English club to win the treble (Premier League, FA Cup, Champions League).

question: Who am I?
clues: I am an Argentine footballer who led my country to World Cup glory in 1986 and scored two of the most famous goals in football history in the same match
One of my goals was scored with my hand, which I called 'a little with the head of Maradona and a little with the hand of God'
The other goal in that match saw me dribble past five English players and the goalkeeper to score what was voted the Goal of the Century
I captained Argentina wearing the number 10 shirt and am regarded as one of the two greatest players of all time
explanation: Maradona's two goals against England in the 1986 World Cup quarter-final encapsulate his contradictory genius — the handball and the masterpiece. He passed away in November 2020.

question: What football competition am I?
clues: I am the most prestigious annual club football competition in the world, contested by the best clubs in Europe
I was rebranded from the 'European Cup' in 1992, introducing a group stage format
My trophy is nicknamed 'the big ears' due to its distinctive large handles
Real Madrid is my most successful participant, having won me 15 times
explanation: The Champions League was first won (under the new format) by Marseille in 1993. The famous anthem — Handel's 'Zadok the Priest' adapted by Tony Britten — plays before every match.

question: Who am I?
clues: I am a Brazilian footballer known worldwide for my dazzling skill, elasticos, and my infectious smile
I scored a stunning free kick against England's David Seaman during the 2002 World Cup, looping the ball into the net from 40 yards
I won the Ballon d'Or in 2005 while playing for FC Barcelona
My brother Roberto Assis was also my agent, and my full name is Anderson Luis de Abreu Oliveira
explanation: Ronaldinho is considered one of the most naturally gifted players ever. He won the Champions League with Barcelona in 2006 and the World Cup with Brazil in 2002.

question: What event am I?
clues: I took place on 18 December 2022 at the Lusail Stadium in Qatar
After 90 minutes, the score was 2-2. One player scored a hat-trick in the second half and extra time to equalise each time
The match ended 3-3 after extra time, with one team completing a stunning comeback from 2-0 down
Argentina ultimately won me 4-2 on penalties to claim their third FIFA World Cup title
explanation: Mbappé's hat-trick brought France back from 2-0 down to force extra time, but Argentina won on penalties. It is widely regarded as the greatest World Cup final ever played.

question: Who am I?
clues: I am an Italian manager who has won the UEFA Champions League as manager on five separate occasions — a record
My first two Champions League titles came with AC Milan in 2003 and 2007
I also managed Chelsea, PSG, and Bayern Munich before taking charge of Real Madrid
My three Champions League titles with Real Madrid came in 2014, 2022, and 2024
explanation: Ancelotti is the most decorated manager in Champions League history with 5 titles. As a player, he also won the European Cup twice with AC Milan (1989, 1990).
